name: Pai Chiao Hsia Ch'iu (Shrimp Balls)
recipeIngredient: 1 1/8 lb Shrimp, shelled, deveined, and peeled
1/2 cup Water chestnuts, blanched and finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Arrange bread cubes in one layer on a baking sheet and let them dry at room temperature overnight.
2. In a food processor, puree the shrimp, transfer to a bowl, and stir in water chestnuts, egg white, lard, wine, ginger, scallion, salt, and cornstarch. Beat vigorously with a wooden spoon until well combined.
3. Form rounded teaspoons of the mixture into balls using hands dipped in cold water.
4. Roll each ball in the bread crumbs, pressing lightly.
5. Arrange the bread-crusted shrimp balls in one layer on the baking sheet.
6. Heat oil in a deep fryer to 375°F (190°C).
7. Fry the shrimp balls in batches, turning them occasionally, for 1-2 minutes or until golden brown.
8. Remove with tongs and drain on paper towels.
9. Return to the hot oil and fry again, turning, until deeply golden.
10. Drain on paper towels before serving.
11. Mix together coarse salt and crushed Szechwan peppercorns for dipping.
(Notice: The attribution line has been removed as per the instructions.)
